items.title: One Action, Two Reference Frames: Compound Cognitive Maps of Object Location
items.author: Benjamin Pitt
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. To navigate complex physical environments, animals keep track of the spatial relations among objects using various reference frames, both body-based (e.g., left/right) and environment-based (e.g., east/west), but how these spatial representations interact ...</article>

items.title: Mapping the Ecology of Risk: 100 Risky Choices of Modern Life
items.author: Renato Frey, Olivia FischerDepartment of Psychology, University of Zurich
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. What are the risky choices people face in our complex and fast-changing world? This article reports on a series of population surveys in Switzerland (N= 4,380) that collected those risky choices that are relevant in people’s everyday lives. Using this ...</article>

items.title: Reply to “A Tendency to Answer Consistently Can Generate Apparent Failures to Learn From Failure”
items.author: Lauren Eskreis-Winkler, Ayelet Fishbach
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. In Eskreis-Winkler and Fishbach (2019), failure stymies learning: People learn less from failure than success. The commentary proposes that the failure to learn from failure could be due to a tendency to respond consistently. Although a consistent ...</article>

items.title: Invariant Recognition Memory Spaces for Real-World Objects Revealed With Signal-Detection Analysis
items.author: Igor Utochkin, Daniil Azarov, Daniil Grigorev
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Recognition memoryrefers to the process of distinguishing between previously experienced and novel events.
